# Source Card: Modified Delphi panel consensus recommendations for management of severe aplastic anemia

## Summary
This peer-reviewed RAND/UCLA modified Delphi consensus (Blood Advances 2024;8(15):3946-3960; 11 US adult and pediatric aplastic-anemia experts) establishes diagnostic-classification cutoffs and hematologic-response definitions for severe aplastic anemia (SAA) applicable across all ages, plus age-thresholded recommendations for excluding inherited bone marrow failure (IBMF) syndromes. For the pediatric CDS module it supplies absolute count/cellularity cutoffs (Camitta criteria), PNH-clone diagnostic-performance thresholds, and an age <=40 y reflex-screening rule. All values are expert-consensus/definition-derived and are absolute cutoffs, NOT pediatric age/sex-specific reference intervals.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) SAA = marrow cellularity <25% (or 25-50% with <30% residual) + >=2 of neutrophils <0.5, platelets <20, reticulocytes <60 (x10^9/L) — locator: Introduction, Camitta paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:d55718d1ec666eca393794844b19ec2182354ab7fa8a20c4190a1a26d2e63978]"
- (ev_002) Very severe AA = SAA criteria but neutrophils <0.2 x10^9/L — locator: Introduction — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:626612d96768f33e479e2dc56f9b8ea6fcb5d01d2c203ea99955debc1608b90a]"
- (ev_003) Complete hematologic response = neutrophils >1 (some trials 1.5) x10^9/L, platelets >100 x10^9/L, Hgb >100 g/L — locator: Subsequent management — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:97e3e67a0a91caf97b89d0cafab55418aee768f5a7285dc669cc46caa209cb29]"
- (ev_004) PNH granulocyte clone >1% ~100% PPV for AA; >0.1% ~95% specificity/91% PPV — locator: Tests to diagnose acquired SAA — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:3e40f8790c655512967da2e717878347246214edeeb26165469284e7503ce285]"
- (ev_005) IBMF exclusion: age <=40 y -> chromosome breakage (FA) + telomere length analysis — locator: IBMF testing paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:ec9b029a4a6039487221902b392c87c3adc8cfbc5eb2ace638f9c01b65f09a80]"
- (ev_006) Supportive-care thresholds (descriptive, not a directive): RBC at Hgb <7 g/L [sic] or symptomatic; platelets <10 (or <50 if bleeding/procedure) x10^9/L — locator: Supportive care paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:ef391b6d1317844f85a1e61c9211e9d8f835eb4c219234e42f7079078c4a3566]".

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: acquired SAA of all ages, pediatric explicitly in scope (panel included 3 pediatric hematologists; pediatric band defined elsewhere in the paper as aged <=20 years). The diagnostic cutoffs are absolute Camitta thresholds applied uniformly across ages — they are NOT CALIPER-style pediatric reference intervals and carry no age/sex partitioning.
- Assay: neutrophil/platelet/reticulocyte counts and hemoglobin from automated hematology analyzers; PNH clone by peripheral-blood flow cytometry (gating/clone-size-sensitivity dependent); FA/telomere assays are functional/genetic, not CBC-derived.
- Lifecycle: consensus published 2024-08; cites the modified Camitta criteria for the SAA/vSAA definitions; no version/retire date declared; no explicit prior-guideline supersession stated.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Yes — defines a scope boundary and referral trigger for the pediatric pathway: any patient aged <=40 years with suspected SAA should be routed to IBMF exclusion (chromosome breakage for Fanconi anemia + telomere length analysis) before or alongside AA management (ev_005); germ-line testing when high index of suspicion. The FA note that 10%-25% of patients have somatic reversion mosaicism (false-negative peripheral-blood breakage test, requiring skin-fibroblast testing) is a further scope caveat that a pediatric module should surface rather than clear a patient on a negative blood test alone.

## Limitations & conflicts
- UNITS-EXTRACTION ARTIFACT (resolved): PMC full-text had rendered the published "× 10^9/L" cell-count units as "× 10⁹/L" (superscript 9 dropped). The affected count-unit quotes (ev_001, ev_002, ev_003, ev_006) were re-verified against the publisher JATS full text (Europe PMC PMC11331724) and corrected to the intact "× 10⁹/L"; UCUM remains `10*9/L`.
- CONFLICT / probable source typo (ev_006): the RBC transfusion trigger is stated verbatim as "Hgb of <7 g/L", which is internally inconsistent with the same paper's use of "Hgb of >100 g/L" for complete response (a <7 g/L hemoglobin is physiologically implausible). The standard and almost-certainly-intended threshold is 7 g/dL (= 70 g/L). Preserved as a conflict, NOT silently corrected; a pediatric CDS converter must resolve the unit before any use and must NOT treat this as an executable transfusion order (recorded here as descriptive supportive-care content only).
- No pediatric-specific numeric partitioning: none of the count cutoffs are stratified by age band, sex, gestational age, or postnatal age; missingness of neonatal/infant-specific values is explicit — the source does not provide them, and none should be inferred.
- Consensus (not GRADE-graded) evidence from an 11-member US-only panel; the authors state it may not generalize internationally and is not intended to supersede individual clinical judgment.
- No conflicts recorded against other source cards in this run at authoring time (conflicts_with empty); the g/L-vs-g/dL issue is intra-source.
